response:
THE DESIGNER IS CREATING A POSTER WITH 8 CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. EACH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL CIRCLES IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH INDIVIDUAL CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS GIVEN BY THE FORMULA πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS. THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R. THE SECOND HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1R, THE THIRD 1.1²R, AND SO ON, UP TO THE 8TH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THEREFORE πR²(1 + 1.1² + 1.1⁴ + 1.1⁶ + 1.1⁸ + 1.1¹⁰ + 1.1¹² + 1.1¹⁴). THIS SERIES CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πR² MULTIPLIED BY THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC PROGRESSION WITH FIRST TERM 1 AND COMMON RATIO 1.21, FOR 8 TERMS. THE SUM OF THIS SERIES IS πR² × [(1.21⁸ - 1)/0.21]. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY THE 8 CIRCLES.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR₅, WHERE R₅ IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. SINCE EACH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS, R₅ = 1.1⁴R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEREFORE 2π × 1.1⁴R. TO PLACE CHARACTERS EVENLY WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH, THE TOTAL CIRCUMFERENCE MUST BE DIVIDED BY 0.5 TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS. THIS GIVES (2π × 1.1⁴R) / 0.5. THIS VALUE MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE FLOOR OF THIS VALUE, ENSURING IT IS AN INTEGER. THE DESIGNER MUST CALCULATE THIS TO PLACE THE TEXT ACCURATELY. THE LETTER I APPEARS 11 TIMES
